compiled_state.title: Depth-tier classifier and standard/deep schema with schema_version field for forward-compat across depth changes
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Scope
Add the 8-dimension complexity rubric that emits manifest_depth, extend the schema with standard/deep tier fields, and establish schema_version forward-compat discriminator.

## Done Definitions
- 8-dimension complexity rubric implemented; emits manifest_depth ∈ {minimal, standard, deep}.
- **Spike flag**: classifier is explicitly marked as a spike (novel mapping with heuristic weights) in the implementation plan; FP auto-fallback in Story 6 hedges against misclassification.
- Standard tier fields: extended_gate_verdicts[], derived_invariants[].
- Deep tier fields: full_trace[], dependency_chain[].
- **Every PRECONDITIONS event (minimal/standard/deep) includes a `schema_version` integer field**. Minimal = 1 (Story 1); standard/deep = 2 (this story). Future field additions increment.
- **Forward-compat reader behavior**: readers treat unknown schema_version with documented fallback — log warning once per (ticket, version), fall back to minimal-tier interpretation of known fields; never reject.
- manifest_depth serves as the de-facto field-set discriminator alongside schema_version (a minimal-tier event at schema_version=2 is valid).
- Classifier invokes writer with appropriate depth tag; writer remains tier-agnostic.
- Unit tests: classifier emits correct tier for each of 8 dimensions at representative values; reader accepts unknown schema_version without rejection; known-tier events round-trip cleanly.
- Contract doc stub in the epic's contracts dir (full contract pinned in Story 7).

## Considerations
- [Maintainability] schema_version is append-log forward-compat insurance — document the evolution policy (bump on breaking field changes; never repurpose).
- [Testing] Classifier is a spike — reserve a generous FP budget (>10%) that Story 6's auto-fallback absorbs until rubric stabilizes.
